steps.title: Isolate the absolute value expression
steps.content: Start with $-4|5x+6|+4=-20$. Subtract 4 from both sides to get $-4|5x+6|=-24$. Divide both sides by -4: $$|5x+6|=6$$

steps.title: Split into two cases using the absolute value definition
steps.content: When $|A| = 6$, the expression inside equals either 6 or $-6$. Set up both cases: $$5x+6=6 \quad \text{or} \quad 5x+6=-6$$

steps.title: Solve both cases
steps.content:
From $5x+6=6$: Subtract 6 gives $5x=0$, so $x=0$.
From $5x+6=-6$: Subtract 6 gives $5x=-12$, so $x=-\frac{12}{5}$.

The two solutions are $x=0$ and $x=-\frac{12}{5}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
